title: Bundle metadata (<type>_meta.yaml)
description: The machine-checkable contract for a bundle's catalog meta. Every templates/<type>/<type>_meta.yaml must validate against this. It describes the template as a library asset and never travels into a filled document. Encodes ADR 0015: a meta declares phase XOR classification, never both, never neither. Adopted by ADR 0016 (from RFC-0001); enforced by check-bundles.py check J, which may use a JSON Schema validator per ADR 0017. Adding, removing, or loosening a field is a deliberate change to this file, reviewed as such - that is the contract working, not a burden.

id.description: The bundle's stable identifier and folder name. Canonical kebab-case (lowercase, single hyphens between alphanumeric segments, no leading/trailing/double hyphen). Equals doc_type.

doc_type.description: The document type this bundle produces. Equals id (a JSON Schema cannot assert cross-field equality; check J does not enforce it, but they must match by convention).

title.description: Human-readable name of the document type, e.g. 'Product Requirements Document'. Must contain a non-whitespace character.

summary.description: One or two sentences: what the document is for, so an agent or reader can select it from this field alone. Must contain a non-whitespace character.

phase.description: The lifecycle phase this artifact belongs to (ADR 0003). Present on lifecycle artifacts; mutually exclusive with classification (ADR 0015).

classification.description: The standing-artifact class for a cross-phase document with no single lifecycle phase (ADR 0015, matching pm-skills). Present on standing artifacts; mutually exclusive with phase.

family.description: The bundle family this type belongs to, e.g. delivery-docs, decision-docs. The controlled family vocabulary is WP-24's job; this field only requires a canonical kebab-case token for now.

sizes_available.description: The size contract (ADR 0010): the variants this bundle ships, using exactly one of the two legal vocabularies, never mixed. A single-size bundle declares one value.

default_size.description: The recommended starting size for this bundle. The schema constrains it to a legal size value; check F enforces the cross-field rule that it must be one of sizes_available (which a JSON Schema cannot express).

sizing_guidance.description: One sentence on when to pick each size, so an agent or author chooses a weight deliberately rather than defaulting blindly.

methodology.description: The methodology the template leans on, e.g. generic, agile-scrum-xp, agile-bdd, methodology-agnostic. Free token, no closed vocabulary yet. Must contain a non-whitespace character.

pairs_with.description: pm-skills skill IDs this template serves. May be empty (a bundle with no paired skill, e.g. rfc). Shape only; cross-repo resolution is check I's job against tools/known-skills.txt.

status.description: Maturity of the bundle as a library asset (adopted from RFC-0001, with draft added for pre-review authoring). draft: under authoring, not yet offered for use. beta: offered for use, gathering feedback. stable: settled. deprecated: no longer recommended.

template_version.description: Semantic version (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) of the template itself, no leading zeros in any segment. check H requires a matching history entry. Bumped only on a real template change.

tags.description: Free-text discovery tags. May be empty, though most bundles carry several.

related_templates.description: Sibling bundle ids this type relates to. Each must name a bundle that exists, or carry the future: prefix for one not built yet (check I resolves them). May be empty.

aliases.description: Other names a reader or agent might use for this document type. Free text (may contain spaces and capitals). May be empty.

catalog_ref.description: Entry number in the deep-research master catalog.

maintainer.description: Who maintains this bundle. Must contain a non-whitespace character.

last_reviewed.description: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) the bundle was last reviewed. The pattern guards structure; format: date (enforced by check J's format checker) rejects impossible calendar dates like 2026-02-30. Quote it in YAML or leave it bare; check J normalizes YAML dates to strings before validating.

license.description: SPDX license identifier for the bundle, e.g. Apache-2.0. Must contain a non-whitespace character.
